reference: Roads, C., 2004. Microsound. The MIT Press.
why: One of the foundational books on granular synthesis, and all methods of manipulating microsounds. It provides general descriptions of the various methods the author but also others in the field have used. It details the general approach and parameterization of all of the proposed methods.
notes: Ch 3: details basic fundamentals of granular synthesis. Details different approaches to making granular clouds, as Roads calls them, which are time sequences where grains are either synchronously, or asynchronously arranged. There are alternative methods to the parameterizatoin of grains, pitch-synchronous, granulation (= grain waveforms from sampled sounds), and abstract and physical models for the composition and granular controls of gs.
